name: Arugula Lemon Pizza

ingredients.item: lemon plus lemon slices for garnish

method: Add yeast to one cup of warm water. Let sit until foamy and there are bubbles on top. In a medium bowl, add flour and salt. Slowly pour yeast water into flour mixture, use your hands to mix. Continue mixing until the dough and water are combined and you can handle the dough. The less flour and the less you knead the dough, the harder the pizza crust will come out. I add flour and knead the dough just a little, not too much, I knead it more after the first rise. Dough should be a little wet. Cover with a dish towel and let rise in a warm place. When dough has risen to double for an hour, punch down, knead for 2 minutes, and drizzle olive oil on top. Cover again and let rise to double another hour. Preheat oven to 460°F. In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon, and salt. Add arugula and toss well. Set aside. Roll out pizza crust with a rolling pin on a semolina-dusted surface, transfer to a parchment-lined 12 inch pizza pan with holes. Bake on bottom rack until bottom is browned evenly. Remove from oven. Preheat oven to broil 500°F and move rack to top. Drizzle olive oil over the crust, I like to rub it in a little. Add parmesan and mozzarella slices and spread evenly over top of crust. Salt & pepper to taste, lots of black pepper on this pizza is a nice touch. Broil on top rack until the crust is charred and the cheeses are melted and bubbly. Remove from oven and spread lemon arugula over the top, garnish with slices of lemon and more cracked black pepper. Serves 2. Serving suggestion: It's a delicious summer pizza that's very Italian! Olé
